2026-03-27T04:19:29.6253189Z Agent: 
2026-03-27T04:19:29.6253450Z Agent: Let me now look at what files I've found and the issues:
2026-03-27T04:19:29.6253787Z Agent: 
2026-03-27T04:19:29.6254084Z Agent: **Issue 1 (Design):** `AzureClouds` enum uses UPPER_SNAKE_CASE values:
2026-03-27T04:19:29.6254469Z Agent: ```typescript
2026-03-27T04:19:29.6255261Z Agent: export enum AzureClouds {
2026-03-27T04:19:29.6255604Z Agent:     AZURE_CHINA_CLOUD = "AZURE_CHINA_CLOUD",
2026-03-27T04:19:29.6255975Z Agent:     AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD = "AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD",
2026-03-27T04:19:29.6256338Z Agent:     AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT = "AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT"
2026-03-27T04:19:29.6256647Z Agent: }
2026-03-27T04:19:29.6256828Z Agent: ```
2026-03-27T04:19:29.6257574Z Agent: Per Azure SDK TypeScript guidelines, enum member names should use PascalCase (e.g., `AzureChinaCloud`, `AzurePublicCloud`, `AzureUsGovernment`).
2026-03-27T04:19:29.6258227Z Agent: 
2026-03-27T04:19:29.6259633Z Agent: **Issue 2 (Design):** `AzureSupportedClouds = \`${AzureClouds}\`` uses a closed template literal type instead of `string`. This creates a type that is equivalent to `"AZURE_CHINA_CLOUD" | "AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD" | "AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT"` but is represented as a template literal. Per guidelines, this should be a union type instead.
